steps.dialogue: Really nice work on this lesson.

You started by reviewing [event:690d15f757440ed4] unit fractions like [event:690d16bf082a67c9]one-fourth. Then you learned that you could combine those unit fractions to build ANY fraction you wanted.

As you do these kinds of problems - adding one unit fraction at a time and counting as you go - your ability to do this with fractions is becoming automatic. That careful counting strategy you developed will help you in future lessons when we explore more complex fraction relationships.
You finished all the problems. Good work!
